PETITIONER: Gena Ram Police Station Ranjeetpura, Bikaner
COURT:
HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE FOR RAJASTHAN AT
JODHPUR
STATUTE: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985

summary: Gena Ram vs State Of Rajasthan (2026:Rj-Jd:16674) on 9 April, 2026. The instant bail application has been filed by the petitioner under Section 483 BNSS. Counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner has been falsely implicated in the present case. It is alleged that upon search, the petitioner was found cultivating poppy plants in his agricultural field. The embargo of Section 37 of the NDPS Act would not come in the way of granting bail to the petitioner. The accused, Gena Ram, shall be released on bail; he furnishes a personal bond in the sum of Rs50,000/reties of two sureties. (At the time of this hearing, the accused was present Lodged In Central Jail, Bikaner.) The bail application is allowed and the accused will return to the High Court in the near future to face trial for the charge of possession of opium poppy plants. The case is adjourned until the next hearing on April 16.
